evals.expected_output: Claude resolves docs/specs/add-user-notifications/spec.md, reads the existing PRD (business sections), preserves all business content unchanged, appends technical sections after a --- separator: functional requirements, permissions, API design with concrete examples, implementation plan with INDEPENDENT/SEQUENTIAL labels and size estimates, test scenarios with specific IDs/endpoints/error codes, and verification tiers. Recommends /build as next step.

assertions.name: resolves-spec-path
assertions.description: Resolves the spec by checking docs/specs/add-user-notifications/spec.md

assertions.name: preserves-business-content
assertions.description: All existing business content (user story, acceptance criteria, goals, scope) is preserved unchanged

assertions.name: adds-separator
assertions.description: Technical sections are added after a --- horizontal rule separator

assertions.name: has-api-design
assertions.description: Includes API design section with concrete request/response JSON examples and error table

assertions.name: has-implementation-plan
assertions.description: Includes implementation plan with sub-tasks listing file paths

assertions.name: tasks-labeled
assertions.description: Each implementation sub-task is labeled INDEPENDENT or SEQUENTIAL

assertions.name: tasks-sized
assertions.description: Each implementation sub-task has a size estimate (small/medium/large)

assertions.name: has-test-scenarios
assertions.description: Includes test scenarios with implementation-level detail (specific IDs, endpoints, error codes)

assertions.name: no-duplicate-acceptance-criteria
assertions.description: Test scenarios complement the existing Gherkin acceptance criteria — no duplication

assertions.name: no-placeholders
assertions.description: No [TBD], [placeholder], TODO, or [Insert X] markers in the output

evals.prompt: We have a race condition in the order processing queue — when two workers pick up the same order within milliseconds, it gets charged twice. Need a spec for the fix.
evals.expected_output: Claude detects no prior PRD exists, extracts feature details from conversation (bug fix, technical nature), runs context loading, selects template-simple.md, generates a full spec with problem/solution, file paths, test scenarios covering the race condition and idempotency, and verification section.

assertions.name: detects-standalone-mode
assertions.description: Recognizes no prior PRD exists and falls back to conversation extraction

assertions.name: classifies-as-bug-fix
assertions.description: Classifies as a bug fix / small change, not a larger feature

assertions.name: selects-simple-template
assertions.description: Uses template-simple.md (bug fix / small change < 1 day)

assertions.name: has-reproduction-detail
assertions.description: Describes the race condition with specific timing and concurrency details

assertions.name: has-file-paths
assertions.description: Lists every file to create or modify with full paths

assertions.name: has-idempotency-test
assertions.description: Test scenarios include an idempotency/duplicate-prevention test case

assertions.name: concrete-error-codes
assertions.description: Error messages have specific codes and text, not generic 'return error'

assertions.name: writes-to-docs-specs
assertions.description: Outputs the spec file to docs/specs/ directory

evals.expected_output: Claude resolves the epic spec, reads the overview spec.md and each story spec from the Story Index, enriches each story file individually with technical sections (API design, data model, implementation plan with INDEPENDENT/SEQUENTIAL labels), preserves all business content in each file, updates the overview only if shared architecture notes are needed. Each story gets its own file paths, test scenarios, and verification section.

assertions.name: reads-overview-and-stories
assertions.description: Reads the epic overview spec.md and each story spec listed in the Story Index

assertions.name: enriches-each-story
assertions.description: Adds technical sections to each individual story spec file, not just the overview

assertions.name: preserves-all-business-content
assertions.description: Business content in every file (overview and stories) is preserved unchanged

assertions.name: per-story-implementation-plans
assertions.description: Each story has its own implementation plan with sub-tasks, not one shared plan

assertions.name: tasks-labeled-per-story
assertions.description: Sub-tasks in each story are labeled INDEPENDENT or SEQUENTIAL

assertions.name: per-story-file-paths
assertions.description: Each story lists the specific files it will create or modify

assertions.name: no-cross-story-duplication
assertions.description: Technical sections don't duplicate content across stories — each story covers only its scope

assertions.name: overview-updated-if-needed
assertions.description: Overview is updated only if shared architecture notes or cross-story dependencies are needed
